source.title: Admissible Spacetime Deflection under Finite Capacity: Lessons from Optical Metrics
source.author: Inacio F. Vasquez

source_claims_recorded: finite capacity is proposed as an admissibility requirement for physically realizable spacetime geometry
realizable metrics support bounded information transport, bounded amplification of gradients, and finite operational resolution
optical metrics provide exact analog geometries for light rays
outward deflection can arise from bounded geometry alone without exotic matter
the optical metric relation is n(r) = alpha(r)^(-1/2)
outward deflection occurs when d_r n(r) > 0, equivalently d_r alpha(r) < 0
a bounded witness profile is n(r) = 1 - epsilon * exp(-r/R), with 0 < epsilon < 1
admissible_use: finite-capacity source map for bounded outward deflection
model-selection analogy for excluding infinite operational amplification
background source for optical-metric admissibility language
